definition: Nature’s neon lights, appearing only when you really can’t find your camera.
When winter decides to throw a snow party and forgets to send an end time.
Wispy clouds that dance in the sky while whispering, 'It might rain, but I'm not telling.'
Sky pillows that gather overhead to gossip about whether they should rain.
A swirling storm that spins faster than your washing machine on its angriest cycle.
Rain that cannot commit to being dramatic but still ruins your hair.
A cloud that got lost and decided to wander around at street level.
Frozen sky marbles that fall down and bully your windshield.
When the sun decides your entire town should become soup.
A massive spinning storm that treats coastlines like poorly arranged furniture.
A frozen spike of doom hanging from rooftops, just waiting to drop on unsuspecting heads.
Sky electricity that flashes dramatically before thunder gives its loud opinion.
Fog’s shy cousin that gently appears and makes everything look mysterious.
Rainy season that turns streets into rivers and umbrellas into boats.
Nature’s colorful arch that teases you with gold but leaves you with wet socks.
Frozen rain that says, ‘Snow wasn’t enough—we need an icy slip hazard too.’
When the sky tosses down flakes like confetti, turning the world into a giant freezer.
A meteorological tantrum where thunder and lightning decide the skies need a mosh pit.
Weather’s golden mood booster, best served with sunglasses and unrealistic optimism.
A storm so dramatic, Shakespeare wrote a play to capture its moody attitude.
The sky’s drumroll after lightning shows off first.
A swirling vortex that flings cows, houses, and your afternoon plans around.
A swirling whirlwind that might steal your hat and your dignity in one gust.
Invisible air on the move, famous for stealing hats and ruining picnics.
